name: Gastroesophageal Reflux
description: Gastroesophageal reflux occurs when gastric acid and bile flow back into the oesophagus, causing oesophagitis. It is associated with anaesthesia, hiatal hernias, and chronic vomiting. It predisposes to oesophageal stricture.
treatment_overview: Proton pump inhibitors (omeprazole), H2 blockers, prokinetics (metoclopramide, cisapride), and small frequent low-fat meals. Treat hiatal hernia surgically if present.
prevention_tips: Withhold food before anaesthesia as directed
Keep head elevated during and after anaesthesia
